zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane De Vauncy於1364年出生在英國坎布利其爾維斯特利 沃特萊斯時，她的父親Edmund De Vauncey25歲，母親Aubigny Creake34歲。她於1392年與Sir Richard Waldegrave在英國素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1399年在英國素福爾可艾德沃德斯同享年35歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可布勒斯 聖 瑪麗。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane De Vauncy于1364年出生在英国坎布利其爾維斯特利 沃特萊斯时，她的父亲Edmund De Vauncey25岁，母亲Aubigny Creake34岁。她于1392年与Sir Richard Waldegrave在英国素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1399年在英国素福爾可艾德沃德斯同享年35岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可布勒斯 聖 瑪麗。

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane De Vauncy was born in 1364, in Westley Waterless, Cambridgeshire, England, her father, Edmund De Vauncey, was 25 and her mother, Aubigny Creake, was 34. She married Sir Richard Waldegrave in 1392, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1399, in Edwardstone, Suffolk, England, at the age of 35, and was buried in Bures Saint Mary, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane De Vauncy est née en 1364, à Westley Waterless, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund De Vauncey, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Aubigny Creake, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Waldegrave en 1392, à Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1399, à Edwardstone, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterrée à Bures Saint Mary, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1392 in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England Sir Richard Waldegrave geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. ja.lifeSummary: Jane De Vauncyが1364年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアウエストリー ワテルレスで生まれた時，父Edmund De Vaunceyは25歳，母Aubigny Creakeは34歳であった。1392年にイギリススフォルクブリー スト エドムンズでSir Richard Waldegraveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1399年にイギリススフォルクエドワードストーンで35歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクブレス サイント マリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Mary The Virgin Churchyard, Bures: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버스 세인트 메리
ko.lifeSummary: Jane De Vauncy(이)가 1364년 영국 캄브리지실르 웨스틀리 왓를레스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund De Vauncey(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Aubigny Creake(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1392년 영국 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 Sir Richard Waldegrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1399년 영국 수폴크 에드워스톤에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버스 세인트 메리에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Waldegrave em 1392, em Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.Saint Mary The Virgin Churchyard, Bures: буресь сеинт мэри, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane De Vauncy родилась в 1364 г. в местности вестли вэитирльсс, камбриджешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund De Vauncey было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Aubigny Creake, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Waldegrave в 1392 г. в местности бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1399 г. в местности эдвардстоун, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 35 лет и была похоронена в местности буресь сеинт мэри, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
